title: T-004 — lp_reservations table migration
prompt:
# T-004 — lp_reservations table migration

PRD: `05-WAREHOUSE-PRD.md` §5.4, §9.4.
Project root: `/Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira`.

## Goal
Create `lp_reservations` table for hard-lock RM-root reservations with the partial-unique constraint preventing parallel reservations.

## Implementation contract
Add `packages/db/src/schema/lp-reservations.ts` per §5.4: id, tenant_id, lp_id, wo_id, wo_material_id, reserved_qty (decimal(14,4)), reservation_type ENUM ('hard_lock'), reserved_at, reserved_by, released_at, released_by, release_reason ENUM ('consumed','cancelled','wo_cancelled','admin_override'). Partial unique: `UNIQUE(lp_id) WHERE released_at IS NULL`. CHECK reserved_qty > 0.

## Files (scope)
- packages/db/src/schema/lp-reservations.ts [create]
- packages/db/migrations/0NN_lp_reservations.sql [create]
- packages/db/src/schema/lp-reservations.test.ts [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given two active reservations (released_at NULL) target the same lp_id, when the second inserts, then DB raises unique violation.
2. Given the second reservation is created after the first sets released_at=NOW(), when committed, then it succeeds.
3. Given INSERT with reserved_qty=0, when committed, then DB raises a CHECK violation.

## Test strategy
- RED: lp-reservations.test.ts covers the three scenarios above with tx rollback.
- Run `pnpm --filter db migrate && pnpm --filter db test lp-reservations`.

## Risk red lines
- Do not drop the partial-unique constraint (V-WH-FEFO-003).
- Do not allow reservations against intermediate LPs at schema level (enforcement is service-layer, but document in TS comment per Q6 revised).
- Do not implement service logic here (T-031 owns).

pipeline_inputs.description: Create lp_reservations table with partial-unique active-reservation constraint per §5.4.
pipeline_inputs.details: Drizzle schema + migration. Partial UNIQUE(lp_id) WHERE released_at IS NULL enforces V-WH-FEFO-003. CHECK reserved_qty > 0. Service logic deferred to T-031.
